Speak or type stream-of-consciousness input across any domain — projects, research, lookups, personal context — and receive parallel, organized results on a live canvas or in audio.**\n4\t\n5\tCLI invocation: `adc`\n6\t\n7\t---\n8\t\n9\t## The Problem\n10\t\n11\tManaging multiple active projects means constant context-switching: open the right tab, re-establish context, ask the question, wait, switch to the next project, repeat. The input channel — typing into a specific session — is also the routing layer, and that conflation is the friction.\n12\t\n13\tBeyond projects: the same friction applies to research, personal notes, lookups, reminders, and anything else that requires knowing where to ask. You shouldn't have to know where to ask.\n14\t\n15\t---\n16\t\n17\t## What aide-de-camp Does\n18\t\n19\tYou speak or type without caring which project, domain, or system you're addressing:\n20\t\n21\t> \"Has the options pipeline caught up? Also what's the state of the IBKR MCP, and remind me what name candidates we had for pdftract.\"\n22\t\n23\taide-de-camp:\n24\t1. Segments the utterance into intent threads and tags each with project and intent type\n25\t2. Dispatches each thread to a parallel agent — one per thread, all running concurrently\n26\t3. Each agent fetches live context (kubectl, git, beads, personal notes) and produces a structured result\n27\t4. Results render as HTML cards on a live canvas, appearing as agents resolve\n28\t5. In audio mode, results are narrated at natural conversational pauses\n29\t\n30\tNo switching. No routing by hand. One surface for everything.\n31\t\n32\t```\n33\t┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n34\t│  Voice / Text Input                                  │\n35\t│  \"has options pipeline caught up? ibkr mcp status?\" │\n36\t└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n37\t                          │\n38\t                          ▼\n39\t               ┌──────────────────┐\n40\t               │   Intent Router  │  ← one LLM call, splits utterance\n41\t               │   (haiku-class)  │    tags each thread: project + intent_type\n42\t               └──────────────────┘\n43\t                          │\n44\t            ┌─────────────┴──────────────┐\n45\t            ▼                            ▼\n46\t     [agent: options-pipeline]   [agent: ibkr-mcp]\n47\t     Fetch → Synthesize           Fetch → Synthesize\n48\t            │                            │\n49\t            ▼                            ▼\n50\t      status card                  status card\n51\t                          │\n52\t                          ▼\n53\t┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n54\t│  Canvas                                             │\n55\t│  ┌─────────────────┐   ┌─────────────────┐         │\n56\t│  │ options-pipeline │   │ ibkr-mcp        │         │\n57\t│  │ [status card]   │   │ [status card]   │         │\n58\t│  └─────────────────┘   └─────────────────┘         │\n59\t└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n60\t```\n61\t\n62\t---\n63\t\n64\t## Key Properties\n65\t\n66\t### Universal scope\n67\tNot a coding assistant. Research, lookups, project status, personal context, reminders, brainstorming — any intent type the router can classify, any agent can handle.\n68\t\n69\t### Parallel dispatch\n70\tEach intent thread runs concurrently. A compound utterance touching five projects takes as long as the slowest single agent, not the sum of all five.\n71\t\n72\t### Self-improving through conversation\n73\tAfter initial bootstrap, the interface improves itself by talking into it:\n74\t\n75\t- \"Always include pod restart count in status cards\" → synthesize prompt updated, hot-reloaded\n76\t- \"You always route 'the feed' to the wrong project\" → registry alias added\n77\t- \"The deploy card is too cluttered\" → UI-regen agent iterates the component\n78\t- \"Start monitoring the pipeline and tell me if it goes behind\" → monitoring rule created\n79\t\n80\tNo IDE switch. No PR. No deployment. The improvement loop collapses into the usage loop.\n81\t\n82\t### Hot-reload everywhere\n83\tAll behavior lives in data — prompt files, YAML registry, HTML component templates — not compiled code. Every artifact is read per-invocation. A change takes effect on the next utterance.\n84\t\n85\t### Two surfaces, one backend\n86\t- **Canvas (multimedia)**: HTML card grid, no voice output, results stream in as they resolve\n87\t- **Audio (mobile/Telegram)**: AI narrates results, no visual output, tool calls are triggers not queries\n88\t- Same result schema. Same session. Switch surfaces mid-session without losing state.\n89\t\n90\t### Human as exception handler\n91\tThe system handles everything automatically. Genuine blockers — decisions only the user can make — are routed to an exception queue and pushed to Telegram or the canvas exception thread. Everything else resolves without interruption.\n92\t\n93\t---\n94\t\n95\t## Architecture Overview\n96\t\n97\t### Hot path (< 3 seconds to first partial result)\n98\t\n99\t```\n100\tVoice/text input\n101\t  → STT (Web Speech API or whisper-stt)\n102\t  → Intent Router (one direct API call, haiku-class)\n103\t  → N parallel Fetch+Synthesize strands (direct API calls, sonnet-class)\n104\t  → SSE stream to canvas\n105\t```\n106\t\n107\tNo Claude Code session startup on the critical path. All direct API calls via ZAI proxy.\n108\t\n109\t### Async path (task beads, not time-critical)\n110\t\n111\t```\n112\tTask intent (research, coding, long-running work)\n113\t  → NEEDLE task bead created\n114\t  → Claude Code worker picks up bead\n115\t  → Bead watcher detects closure\n116\t  → Result pushed to canvas via SSE or Telegram\n117\t```\n118\t\n119\t### Self-improvement path (async, off critical path)\n120\t\n121\t```\n122\tFeedback (explicit instruction or implicit signal)\n123\t  → Self-modification agent (Claude Code via NEEDLE)\n124\t  → Reads artifact → generates update → shows diff\n125\t  → User approves → artifact written → hot-reloaded\n126\t```\n127\t\n128\t### Agent runtimes\n129\t\n130\t| Runtime | Components |\n131\t|---------|-----------|\n132\t| Realtime API | Voice model (persistent session, voice I/O) |\n133\t| Direct API via ZAI | Intent router, Synthesize strand, Escalate strand |\n134\t| Deterministic code | Fetch strand (kubectl/git/br execution), Bead watcher |\n135\t| Claude Code via NEEDLE | UI-regen agent, Self-modification agent, Background analysis, Task beads |\n136\t\n137\t---\n138\t\n139\t## Session and State Model\n140\t\n141\t**Session**: surface-agnostic persistent entity. The same session is accessible from the canvas, Telegram, or audio mode simultaneously. Switching surfaces does not reset context.\n142\t\n143\t**Topic**: a persistent concern (a project, a research thread, a recurring question). The canvas shows one card per active topic, updated in place as new results arrive.\n144\t\n145\t**Result**: stored structured data (`{data, summary, urgency}`). Persists in the session store.\n146\t\n147\t**Card**: ephemeral rendering artifact. Generated from a result by the UI-regen agent using a component template. Not stored — regenerated as needed.\n148\t\n149\t**Component library**: versioned HTML/CSS templates generated by the UI-regen agent from actual usage. Starts empty, grows organically. Components are iterated when feedback (explicit or implicit) indicates they're serving poorly.\n150\t\n151\t---\n152\t\n153\t## Self-Improvement: What Can Be Changed Through Conversation\n154\t\n155\t| Category | Example instruction | Artifact updated |\n156\t|----------|--------------------|--------------------|\n157\t| Result format | \"Always include restart count in pod status\" | Synthesize strand prompt |\n158\t| Visual layout | \"The deploy card is too cluttered\" | Component iterated |\n159\t| Routing | \"'The feed' means options-aggregator\" | Project registry alias |\n160\t| Compound workflows | \"Add a workflow for deploying IBKR MCP\" | Registry workflow template |\n161\t| Monitoring | \"Watch the pipeline and alert if behind\" | Topic monitoring config |\n162\t| Voice behavior | \"Summaries are too long for audio\" | Voice model prompt |\n163\t| Urgency | \"Don't push pipeline updates unless critical\" | Urgency classifier prompt |\n164\t| New project | \"Add pdftract to the registry\" | Project registry |\n165\t| Exception handling | \"Auto-approve deploys to staging\" | Exception routing rules |\n166\t\n167\t---\n168\t\n169\t## CLI\n170\t\n171\t`adc` is the CLI invocation for aide-de-camp.\n172\t\n173\t```bash\n174\t# Dispatch a text utterance (bypasses web UI)\n175\tadc dispatch \"has the options pipeline caught up?\"\n176\t\n177\t# Query a specific topic\n178\tadc ask \"what name candidates did we have for pdftract?\"\n179\t\n180\t# Check active session status\n181\tadc status\n182\t\n183\t# List active topics\n184\tadc topics\n185\t\n186\t# Show exception queue\n187\tadc exceptions\n188\t```\n189\t\n190\t---\n191\t\n192\t## Repository Structure\n193\t\n194\t```\n195\taide-de-camp/\n196\t├── README.md\n197\t├── docs/\n198\t│   ├── research/          ← architecture and design research\n199\t│   │   ├── overview.md          — system concept, components, open questions\n200\t│   │   ├── agent-runtimes.md    — which runtime powers each component\n201\t│   │   ├── component-library.md — cached, versioned, self-growing UI dispatch-architecture.md — queue-based dispatch, scaling path\n205\t│   │   ├── execution-model.md   — live vs. task profile, bead watcher\n206\t│   │   ├── interaction-modes.md — canvas vs. audio surfaces\n207\t│   │   ├── mayor-parallel.md    — parallel Mayor pattern, Gas City validation\n208\t│   │   ├── mayor-parallel-gascity-update.md — May 2026 Gas City state\n209\t│   │   ├── needle-strands.md    — corrected strand model, dispatch waterfall\n210\t│   │   ├── primitives.md        — what uses beads vs. session store\n211\t│   │   ├── resource-reuse.md    — existing ├── session-model.md     — surface routing, DLQ exception model\n215\t│   │   └── topic-model.md       — topic/intent/result/card hierarchy\n216\t│   ├── plan/\n217\t│   │   └── roadmap.md           — phased implementation plan (Phase 0–4)\n218\t│   └── notes/\n219\t│       └── naming.md            — why aide-de-camp, why adc\n220\t```\n221\t\n222\t---\n223\t\n224\t## What Requires Code vs. Conversation\n225\t\n226\t### Requires a code change and deploy\n227\t- New context-fetch integrations (new APIs, new data sources)\n228\t- New tool implementations for NEEDLE workers\n229\t- Initial bootstrap (Phase 0 implementation)\n230\t\n231\t### Reachable through conversation after bootstrap\n232\t- All prompt tuning (router, synthesize, voice model, urgency classifier)\n233\t- Project registry (aliases, new projects, workflow templates)\n234\t- Component iteration (layout, fields, visual design)\n235\t- Monitoring rules and urgency thresholds\n236\t- Exception routing configuration\n237\t\n238\t---\n239\t\n240\t## Relationship to Existing Infrastructure\n241\t\n242\taide-de-camp is built on top of existing infrastructure, not alongside it:\n243\t\n244\t- **NEEDLE workers** — task beads unchanged; aide-de-camp adds a bead watcher to route closed-bead results to the canvas\n245\t- **telegram-claude-bridge** — reused as always-available surface fallback and HUMAN bead push\n246\t- **whisper-stt** — already deployed on ardenone-cluster; used as STT backend in audio mode\n247\t- **ZAI proxy** (`llm-proxy.ardenone.com`) — all direct API calls (router, synthesize, aide-de-camp\n\n**A universal personal interface. Speak or type stream-of-consciousness input across any domain — projects, research, lookups, personal context — and receive parallel, organized results on a live canvas or in audio.**\n\nCLI invocation: `adc`\n\n---\n\n## The Problem\n\nManaging multiple active projects means constant context-switching: open the right tab, re-establish context, ask the question, wait, switch to the next project, repeat. The input channel — typing into a specific session — is also the routing layer, and that conflation is the friction.\n\nBeyond projects: the same friction applies to research, personal notes, lookups, reminders, and anything else that requires knowing where to ask. You shouldn't have to know where to ask.\n\n---\n\n## What aide-de-camp Does\n\nYou speak or type without caring which project, domain, or system you're addressing:\n\n> \"Has the options pipeline caught up? Also what's the state of the IBKR MCP, and remind me what name candidates we had for pdftract.\"\n\naide-de-camp:\n1. Segments the utterance into intent threads and tags each with project and intent type\n2. Dispatches each thread to a parallel agent — one per thread, all running concurrently\n3. Each agent fetches live context (kubectl, git, beads, personal notes) and produces a structured result\n4. Results render as HTML cards on a live canvas, appearing as agents resolve\n5. In audio mode, results are narrated at natural conversational pauses\n\nNo switching. No routing by hand. One surface for everything.\n\n```\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│  Voice / Text Input                                  │\n│  \"has options pipeline caught up? ibkr mcp status?\" │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n                          │\n                          ▼\n               ┌──────────────────┐\n               │   Intent Router  │  ← one LLM call, splits utterance\n               │   (haiku-class)  │    tags each thread: project + intent_type\n               └──────────────────┘\n                          │\n            ┌─────────────┴──────────────┐\n            ▼                            ▼\n     [agent: options-pipeline]   [agent: ibkr-mcp]\n     Fetch → Synthesize           Fetch → Synthesize\n            │                            │\n            ▼                            ▼\n      status card                  status card\n                          │\n                          ▼\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│  Canvas                                             │\n│  ┌─────────────────┐   ┌─────────────────┐         │\n│  │ options-pipeline │   │ ibkr-mcp        │         │\n│  │ [status card]   │   │ [status card]   │         │\n│  └─────────────────┘   └─────────────────┘         │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n---\n\n## Key Properties\n\n### Universal scope\nNot a coding assistant. Research, lookups, project status, personal context, reminders, brainstorming — any intent type the router can classify, any agent can handle.\n\n### Parallel dispatch\nEach intent thread runs concurrently. A compound utterance touching five projects takes as long as the slowest single agent, not the sum of all five.\n\n### Self-improving through conversation\nAfter initial bootstrap, the interface improves itself by talking into it:\n\n- \"Always include pod restart count in status cards\" → synthesize prompt updated, hot-reloaded\n- \"You always route 'the feed' to the wrong project\" → registry alias added\n- \"The deploy card is too cluttered\" → UI-regen agent iterates the component\n- \"Start monitoring the pipeline and tell me if it goes behind\" → monitoring rule created\n\nNo IDE switch. No PR. No deployment. The improvement loop collapses into the usage loop.\n\n### Hot-reload everywhere\nAll behavior lives in data — prompt files, YAML registry, HTML component templates — not compiled code. Every artifact is read per-invocation. A change takes effect on the next utterance.\n\n### Two surfaces, one backend\n- **Canvas (multimedia)**: HTML card grid, no voice output, results stream in as they resolve\n- **Audio (mobile/Telegram)**: AI narrates results, no visual output, tool calls are triggers not queries\n- Same result schema. Same session. Switch surfaces mid-session without losing state.\n\n### Human as exception handler\nThe system handles everything automatically. Genuine blockers — decisions only the user can make — are routed to an exception queue and pushed to Telegram or the canvas exception thread. Everything else resolves without interruption.\n\n---\n\n## Architecture Overview\n\n### Hot path (< 3 seconds to first partial result)\n\n```\nVoice/text input\n  → STT (Web Speech API or whisper-stt)\n  → Intent Router (one direct API call, haiku-class)\n  → N parallel Fetch+Synthesize strands (direct API calls, sonnet-class)\n  → SSE stream to canvas\n```\n\nNo Claude Code session startup on the critical path. All direct API calls via ZAI proxy.\n\n### Async path (task beads, not time-critical)\n\n```\nTask intent (research, coding, long-running work)\n  → NEEDLE task bead created\n  → Claude Code worker picks up bead\n  → Bead watcher detects closure\n  → Result pushed to canvas via SSE or Telegram\n```\n\n### Self-improvement path (async, off critical path)\n\n```\nFeedback (explicit instruction or implicit signal)\n  → Self-modification agent (Claude Code via NEEDLE)\n  → Reads artifact → generates update → shows diff\n  → User approves → artifact written → hot-reloaded\n```\n\n### Agent runtimes\n\n| Runtime | Components |\n|---------|-----------|\n| Realtime API | Voice model (persistent session, voice I/O) |\n| Direct API via ZAI | Intent router, Synthesize strand, Escalate strand |\n| Deterministic code | Fetch strand (kubectl/git/br execution), Bead watcher |\n| Claude Code via NEEDLE | UI-regen agent, Self-modification agent, Background analysis, Task beads |\n\n---\n\n## Session and State Model\n\n**Session**: surface-agnostic persistent entity. The same session is accessible from the canvas, Telegram, or audio mode simultaneously. Switching surfaces does not reset context.\n\n**Topic**: a persistent concern (a project, a research thread, a recurring question). The canvas shows one card per active topic, updated in place as new results arrive.\n\n**Result**: stored structured data (`{data, summary, urgency}`). Persists in the session store.\n\n**Card**: ephemeral rendering artifact. Generated from a result by the UI-regen agent using a component template. Not stored — regenerated as needed.\n\n**Component library**: versioned HTML/CSS templates generated by the UI-regen agent from actual usage. Starts empty, grows organically. Components are iterated when feedback (explicit or implicit) indicates they're serving poorly.\n\n---\n\n## Self-Improvement: What Can Be Changed Through Conversation\n\n| Category | Example instruction | Artifact updated |\n|----------|--------------------|--------------------|\n| Result format | \"Always include restart count in pod status\" | Synthesize strand prompt |\n| Visual layout | \"The deploy card is too cluttered\" | Component iterated |\n| Routing | \"'The feed' means options-aggregator\" | Project registry alias |\n| Compound workflows | \"Add a workflow for deploying IBKR MCP\" | Registry workflow template |\n| Monitoring | \"Watch the pipeline and alert if behind\" | Topic monitoring config |\n| Voice behavior | \"Summaries are too long for audio\" | Voice model prompt |\n| Urgency | \"Don't push pipeline updates unless critical\" | Urgency classifier prompt |\n| New project | \"Add pdftract to the registry\" | Project registry |\n| Exception handling | \"Auto-approve deploys to staging\" | Exception routing rules |\n\n---\n\n## CLI\n\n`adc` is the CLI invocation for aide-de-camp.\n\n```bash\n# Dispatch a text utterance (bypasses web UI)\nadc dispatch \"has the options pipeline caught up?\"\n\n# Query a specific topic\nadc ask \"what name candidates did we have for pdftract?\"\n\n# Check active session status\nadc status\n\n# List active topics\nadc topics\n\n# Show exception queue\nadc exceptions\n```\n\n---\n\n## Repository Structure\n\n```\naide-de-camp/\n├── README.md\n├── docs/\n│   ├── research/          ← architecture and design research\n│   │   ├── overview.md          — system concept, components, open questions\n│   │   ├── agent-runtimes.md    — which runtime powers each component\n│   │   ├── component-library.md — cached, versioned, self-growing UI components\n│   │   ├── context-model.md     — shallow registry + on-demand fetch\n│   │   ├── cross-project-intents.md — project graph, workflow templates\n│   │   ├── dispatch-architecture.md — queue-based dispatch, scaling path\n│   │   ├── execution-model.md   — live vs. task profile, bead watcher\n│   │   ├── interaction-modes.md — canvas vs. audio surfaces\n│   │   ├── mayor-parallel.md    — parallel Mayor pattern, Gas City validation\n│   │   ├── mayor-parallel-gascity-update.md — May 2026 Gas City state\n│   │   ├── needle-strands.md    — corrected strand model, dispatch waterfall\n│   │   ├── primitives.md        — what uses beads vs. session store\n│   │   ├── resource-reuse.md    — existing infrastructure reused\n│   │   ├── responsiveness.md    — latency, awareness, conversational quality\n│   │   ├── self-improvement.md  — hot-reload, feedback loop, safety model\n│   │   ├── session-model.md     — surface routing, DLQ exception model\n│   │   └── topic-model.md       — topic/intent/result/card hierarchy\n│   ├── plan/\n│   │   └── roadmap.md           — phased implementation plan (Phase 0–4)\n│   └── notes/\n│       └── naming.md            — why aide-de-camp, why adc\n```\n\n---\n\n## What Requires Code vs. Conversation\n\n### Requires a code change and deploy\n- New context-fetch integrations (new APIs, new data sources)\n- New tool implementations for NEEDLE workers\n- Initial bootstrap (Phase 0 implementation)\n\n### Reachable through conversation after bootstrap\n- All prompt tuning (router, synthesize, voice model, urgency classifier)\n- Project registry (aliases, new projects, workflow templates)\n- Component iteration (layout, fields, visual design)\n- Monitoring rules and urgency thresholds\n- Exception routing configuration\n\n---\n\n## Relationship to Existing Infrastructure\n\naide-de-camp is built on top of existing infrastructure, not alongside it:\n\n- **NEEDLE workers** 'created', 'updated', 'closed', 'commented'\n    timestamp: int\n    data: dict\n\n\nclass BeadWatcher:\n    \"\"\"\n    Watches for bead events and routes results to surfaces.\n\n    Integration with br CLI (beads_rust):\n    - Reads beads JSONL file for closed beads\n    - Extracts session_id and surface_id from bead metadata\n    - Pushes results to active surfaces via SSE\n    - Falls back to Telegram if no surface available\n    \"\"\"\n\n    CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS = 5\n    BEADS_JSONL = \".beads/beads.jsonl\"\n\n    def __init__(self, store: SessionStore, router: SurfaceRouter):\n        self.store = store\n        self.router = router\n        self._running = False\n        self._task: Optional[asyncio.Task] = None\n        self._last_position = 0  # Position in beads.jsonl\n        self._processed_beads: set[str] = set()\n\n    async def start(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Start the bead watcher daemon.\"\"\"\n        self._running = True\n        self._task = asyncio.create_task(self._watch_loop())\n        logger.info(\"Bead watcher started\")\n\n    async def stop(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Stop the bead watcher daemon.\"\"\"\n        self._running = False\n        if self._task:\n            self._task.cancel()\n            try:\n                await self._task\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                pass\n        logger.info(\"Bead watcher stopped\")\n\n    async def _watch_loop(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Main watch loop - checks for new bead events.\"\"\"\n        while self._running:\n            try:\n                await self._check_for_events()\n                await asyncio.sleep(self.CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS)\n            except asyncio.CancelledError:\n                break\n            except Exception as e:\n                logger.error(f\"Error in bead watch loop: {e}\", exc_info=True)\n                await asyncio.sleep(self.CHECK_INTERVAL_SECONDS)\n\n    async def _check_for_events(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Check for new bead events and process them.\"\"\"\n        beads_path = Path(self.BEADS_JSONL)\n        if not beads_path.exists():\n            return\n\n        # Read new lines from beads.jsonl\n        events = await self._read_new_bead_events(beads_path)\n\n        for event in events:\n            await self._process_bead_event(event)\n\n    async def _read_new_bead_events(self, beads_path: Path) -> list[BeadEvent]:\n        \"\"\"Read new bead events from beads.jsonl.\"\"\"\n        events = []\n\n        try:\n            with open(beads_path, \"r\") as f:\n                # Seek to last position\n                f.seek(self._last_position)\n                new_lines = f.readlines()\n\n                # Update position\n                self._last_position = f.tell()\n\n                for line in new_lines:\n                    if not line.strip():\n                        continue\n\n                    try:\n                        bead = json.loads(line)\n                        bead_id = bead.get(\"id\")\n                        status = bead.get(\"status\")\n\n                        if not bead_id or bead_id in self._processed_beads:\n                            continue\n\n                        # Process closed beads\n                        if status == \"closed\":\n                            events.append(BeadEvent(\n                                bead_id=bead_id,\n                                event_type=\"closed\",\n                                timestamp=int(datetime.now().timestamp()),\n                                data=bead,\n                            ))\n                            self._processed_beads.add(bead_id)\n\n                    except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n                        logger.warning(f\"Failed to parse bead JSON: {e}\")\n\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Error reading beads file: {e}\")\n\n        return events\n\n    async def _process_bead_event(self, event: BeadEvent) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Process a bead event and route to surfaces.\"\"\"\n        bead_data = event.data\n\n        # Extract metadata\n        metadata = bead_data.get(\"metadata\", {})\n        session_id = metadata.get(\"session_id\")\n        surface_id = metadata.get(\"origin_surface_id\")\n\n        if not session_id:\n            # No session_id - can't route\n            logger.warning(f\"Bead {event.bead_id} has no session_id in metadata\")\n            return\n\n        # Determine target surface\n        decision = await self.router.route_result(\n            session_id=session_id,\n            origin_surface_id=surface_id,\n            urgency=metadata.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n        )\n\n        # Extract result from bead\n        result = await self._extract_result_from_bead(bead_data, session_id)\n\n        if not result:\n            logger.warning(f\"Could not extract result from bead {event.bead_id}\")\n            return\n\n        # Route to target surfaces\n        if decision.target_surfaces:\n            for surface in decision.target_surfaces:\n                if surface.type == \"telegram\":\n                    await self._send_to_telegram(result, session_id)\n                else:\n                    # SSE broadcast\n                    await broadcast_result(\n                        result=result,\n                        session_id=session_id,\n                        target_surface_id=surface.id,\n                    )\n        elif decision.fallback_used:\n            # Fallback to Telegram\n            await self._send_to_telegram(result, session_id)\n        else:\n            # No surface available - result stays in queue\n            logger.info(f\"No surface available for bead {event.bead_id}, result queued\")\n\n    async def _extract_result_from_bead(self, bead: dict, session_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Extract result data from a closed bead.\"\"\"\n        # Try to extract structured result from bead body\n        body = bead.get(\"body\", \"\")\n        metadata = bead.get(\"metadata\", {})\n\n        # If metadata already has result structure, use it\n        if \"result\" in metadata:\n            return metadata[\"result\"]\n\n        # Try to parse result from body\n        # For now, create a basic result from the bead\n        return {\n            \"id\": bead.get(\"id\"),\n            \"type\": \"bead_result\",\n            \"summary\": body[:200] if len(body) > 200 else body,  # Truncate for summary\n            \"data\": {\n                \"bead_id\": bead.get(\"id\"),\n                \"title\": bead.get(\"title\"),\n                \"type\": bead.get(\"type\"),\n                \"body\": body,\n                \"status\": bead.get(\"status\"),\n            },\n            \"urgency\": metadata.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\"),\n            \"created_at\": int(datetime.now().timestamp()),\n            \"surfaced_at\": int(datetime.now().timestamp()),\n        }\n\n    async def _send_to_telegram(self, result: dict, session_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Send result to Telegram via telegram-claude-bridge.\"\"\"\n        try:\n            # telegram-claude-bridge runs on Tailscale mesh\n            async with httpx.AsyncClient() as client:\n                response = await client.post(\n                    \"http://telegram-claude-bridge:8000/send_message\",\n                    json={\n                        \"session_id\": session_id,\n                        \"message\": self._format_telegram_message(result),\n                    },\n                    timeout=5.0,\n                )\n                if response.status_code == 200:\n                    logger.info(f\"Sent result to Telegram for session {session_id}\")\n                else:\n                    logger.warning(f\"Failed to send to Telegram: {response.status_code}\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            logger.error(f\"Error sending to Telegram: {e}\")\n\n    def _format_telegram_message(self, result: dict) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Format result as Telegram message.\"\"\"\n        summary = result.get(\"summary\", \"Result available\")\n        urgency = result.get(\"urgency\", \"normal\")\n\n        emoji_map = {\n            \"critical\": \"🚨\",\n            \"high\": \"⚠️\",\n            \"normal\": \"📌\",\n            \"low\": \"💬\",\n        }\n\n        emoji = emoji_map.get(urgency, \"📌\")\n\n        # db.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL\")\n173\t\n174\t            # Create schema\n175\t            await db.executescript(SCHEMA_SQL)\n176\t            await db.commit()\n177\t\n178\t    async def aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n182\t            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)\")\n183\t\n184\t    # Session operations\n185\t    async def create_session(self) -> (session_id, now, now)\n193\t            )\n194\t            await db.commit()\n195\t        return session_id\n196\t\n197\t    async def get_session(self, session_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n198\t Returns (topic_id, created).\"\"\"\n451\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n452\t            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n453\t            # Try to find existing topic in session scope\n454\t            async with db.execute(\n455\t                \"\"\"SELECT id FROM topics\n456\t                   WHERE label = ? AND scope = 'session' AND session_id = ? AND archived_at IS NULL\n457\t                   LIMIT 1\"\"\",\n458\t                (label, session_id)\n459\t            ) as cursor:\n460\t                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n461\t                if row:\n462\t                    return row[0], False\n463\t\n464\t        # Create new topic\n465\t        topic_id = await self.create_topic(label, topic_type, project_slugs, \"session\", session_id)\n466\t        return topic_id, True\n467\t\n468\t    async def update_topic_activity(self, topic_id: str) -> None:\n469\t        \"\"\"Update topic last_active timestamp.\"\"\"\n470\t        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n471\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n472\t            await db.execute(\n473\t                \"UPDATE topics SET last_active = ? AND status IN ('pending', 'dispatched')\",\n524\t                (session_id,)\n525\t            ))[0][0]\n526\t\n527\t            # Get new results (surfaced after last surface disconnect)\n528\t            # For now, return count of results in last hour\n529\t            one_hour_ago = int(datetime.now().timestamp()) - 3600\n530\t            new_results = (await db.execute_fetchall(\n531\t                \"\"\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM results\n532\t                   WHERE session_id = ? AND created_at > ?\"\"\",\n533\t                (session_id, one_hour_ago)\n534\t            ))[0][0]\n535\t\n536\t            # Get exceptions (HUMAN beads or high urgency items)\n537\t            exceptions = (await db.execute_fetchall(\n538\t                \"\"\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM results\n539\t                   WHERE session_id = ? AND urgency IN ('critical', 'high') AND acked_at IS NULL\"\"\",\n540\t                (session_id,)\n541\t            ))[0][0]\n542\t\n543\t            return {\n544\t                \"pending_intents\": pending_count,\n545\t                \"new_results\": new_results,\n546\t                \"unresolved_exceptions\": exceptions,\n547\t            }\n548\t\n549\t    # Topic context cache operations\n550\t    async def set_topic_context(\n551\t        self,\n552\t        topic_id: str,\n553\t        context_data: dict,\n554\t        ttl_seconds: int = 600,  # Default 10 minutes\n555\t    ) -> None:\n556\t        \"\"\"Cache pre-fetched context for a topic.\"\"\"\n557\t        import json\n558\t        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n559\t        expires_at = now + ttl_seconds\n560\t\n561\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n562\t            await db.execute(\n563\t                \"\"\"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO topic_context_cache\n564\t                   (topic_id, context_data, fetched_at, expires_at)\n565\t                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n566\t                (topic_id, json.dumps(context_data), now, expires_at)\n567\t            )\n568\t            await db.commit()\n569\t\n570\t    async def get_topic_context(self, topic_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n571\t        \"\"\"Get cached context for a topic if still valid.\"\"\"\n572\t        import json\n573\t        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n574\t\n575\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n576\t            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n577\t            async with db.execute(\n578\t                \"\"\"SELECT context_data, expires_at FROM topic_context_cache\n579\t                   WHERE topic_id = ? AND expires_at > ?\"\"\",\n580\t                (topic_id, now)\n581\t            ) as cursor:\n582\t                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n583\t                if row:\n584\t                    return {\n585\t                        \"context\": json.loads(row[\"context_data\"]),\n586\t                        \"expires_at\": row[\"expires_at\"],\n587\t                    }\n588\t        return None\n589\t\n590\t    async def invalidate_topic_context(self, topic_id: str) -> None:\n591\t        \"\"\"Invalidate cached context for a topic.\"\"\"\n592\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n593\t            await db.execute(\n594\t                \"DELETE FROM topic_context_cache WHERE topic_id = ?\",\n595\t                (topic_id,)\n596\t            )\n597\t            await db.commit()\n598\t\n599\t    async def cleanup_expired_context(self) -> int:\n600\t        \"\"\"Remove expired context cache entries. Returns count of deleted entries.\"\"\"\n601\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n602\t            cursor = await db.execute(\n603\t                \"DELETE FROM topic_context_cache WHERE expires_at <= ?\",\n604\t                (int(datetime.now().timestamp()),)\n605\t            )\n606\t            await db.commit()\n607\t            return cursor.rowcount\n608\t\n609\t    async def get_active_topic_ids(self) -> list[str]:\n610\t        \"\"\"Get IDs of all active topics (recently active, not archived).\"\"\"\n611\t        # Active topics are those with activity in last hour\n612\t        one_hour_ago = int(datetime.now().timestamp()) - 3600\n613\t\n614\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n615\t            async with db.execute(\n616\t                \"\"\"SELECT id FROM topics\n617\t                   WHERE last_active > ? AND archived_at IS NULL\"\"\",\n618\t                (one_hour_ago,)\n619\t            ) as cursor:\n620\t                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n621\t                return [row[0] for row in rows]\n622\t\n623\t    # Feedback signal operations\n624\t    async def create_feedback_signal(\n625\t        self,\n626\t        signal_id: str,\n627\t        signal_type: str,\n628\t        session_id: str,\n629\t        result_id: str | None = None,\n630\t        topic_id: str | None = None,\n631\t        timestamp: int | None = None,\n632\t        data: dict | None = None,\n633\t        surface_type: str | None = None,\n634\t    ) -> str:\n635\t        \"\"\"Create a new feedback signal and return its ID.\"\"\"\n636\t        import json\n637\t        now =              (id, signal_type, session_id, result_id, topic_id, timestamp, data, surface_type)\n642\t                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n643\t                (\n644\t                    signal_id, signal_type, session_id, result_id,\n645\t                    topic_id, timestamp or now, json.dumps(data or {}),\n646\t                    surface_type\n647\t                )\n648\t            )\n649\t            await db.commit()\n650\t        return signal_id\n651\t\n652\t    async def get_unprocessed_signals(\n653\t        self,\n654\t        signal_type: str | None = None,\n655\t        limit: int = 100,\n656\t    ) -> list[dict]:\n657\t        \"\"\"Get unprocessed feedback signals for background analysis.\"\"\"\n658\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n659\t            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n660\t            query = \"\"\"SELECT * FROM feedback_signals WHERE processed = 0\"\"\"\n661\t            params = []\n662\t\n663\t            if signal_type:\n664\t                query += \" AND signal_type = ?\"\n665\t                params.append(signal_type)\n666\t\n667\t            query += \" ORDER BY timestamp ASC LIMIT ?\"\n668\t            params.append(limit)\n669\t\n670\t            async with db.execute(query, params) as cursor:\n671\t                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n672\t                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n673\t\n674\t    async def mark_signals_processed(\n675\t        self,\n676\t        signal_ids: list[str],\n677\t    ) -> None:\n678\t        \"\"\"Mark feedback signals as processed.\"\"\"\n679\t        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n680\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n681\t            for signal_id in signal_ids:\n682\t                await db.execute(\n683\t                    \"UPDATE feedback_signals SET processed = 1, processed_at = ? WHERE id = ?\",\n684\t                    (now, signal_id)\n685\t                )\n686\t            await db.commit()\n687\t\n688\t    async def get_session_feedback_summary(\n689\t        self,\n690\t        session_id: str,\n691\t        since: int | None = None,\n692\t    ) -> dict:\n693\t        \"\"\"Get a summary of feedback signals for a session.\"\"\"\n694\t        one_hour_ago = int(datetime.now().timestamp()) - 3600\n695\t\n696\t        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n697\t            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n698\t\n699\t            # Get signal counts by type\n700\t            since_clause = since or one_hour_ago\n701\t            async with db.execute(\n702\t                \"\"\"SELECT signal_type, COUNT(*) as count\n703\t                   FROM feedback_signals\n704\t                   WHERE session_id = ? AND timestamp >= ?\n705\t                   GROUP BY signal_type\"\"\",\n706\t                (session_id, since_clause)\n707\t            ) as cursor:\n708\t                type_counts = {row[\"signal_type\"]: row[\"count\"] for row in await cursor.fetchall()}\n709\t\n710\t            # Get average ack delay\n711\t            async with db.execute(\n712\t                \"\"\"SELECT AVG(CAST(data->>'ack_delay_seconds' AS INTEGER)) as avg_delay\n713\t                   FROM feedback_signals\n714\t                   WHERE session_id = ? ('project', 'research', 'personal', 'exception', 'compound')) DEFAULT 'adhoc',\n    project_slugs TEXT,  -- JSON array\n    scope        TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(scope IN ('session', 'cross-session',       async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            # Enable WAL mode for concurrent reads during writes\n            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL\")\n            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA synchronous=NORMAL\")\n\n            # Create schema\n            await db.executescript(SCHEMA_SQL)\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def close(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Close database connection pool.\"\"\"\n        # aiosqlite uses connection-per-context, so just ensure checkpoint\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\"PRAGMA wal_checkpoint(TRUNCATE)\")\n\n    # Session operations\n    async def create_session(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new session and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        session_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"INSERT INTO sessions (id, created_at, last_active) VALUES (?, ?, ?)\",\n                (session_id, now, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return session_id\n\n    async def get_session(self, with db.execute(\n                \"SELECT * FROM sessions WHERE id = ?\", (session_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                return dict(row) if WHERE id = ?\",\n                (now, session_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def set_primary_surface(self, session_id: str, surface_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Set the primary surface for a session.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE sessions SET primary_surface_id = ? session_id, type, state, always_available, last_seen)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, 'active', ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (surface_id, session_id, surface_type, 1 if always_available else 0, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return surface_id\n\n    async def update_surface_heartbeat(self, surface_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update surface last_seen last_seen = ?, state = 'active' WHERE id = ?\",\n                (now, surface_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def mark_surface_disconnected(self, surface_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Mark a surface as disconnected.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE surfaces SET state = 'disconnected' WHERE id = ?\",\n                (surface_id,)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_active_surfaces(self, session_id: str) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get all active surfaces for a session, ordered by last_seen.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM surfaces\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND state IN ('active', 'idle')\n                   ORDER BY last_seen DESC\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    async def get_fallback_surface(self) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get the always-available fallback surface (Telegram).\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM surfaces\n                   WHERE always_available = 1 AND state != 'disconnected'\n                   LIMIT 1\"\"\"\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                return dict(row) if row else None\n\n    # Utterance operations\n    async def create_utterance(self, session_id: str, raw_text: str) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new utterance and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        utterance_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"INSERT INTO utterances (id, session_id, raw_text, created_at) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\",\n                (utterance_id, session_id, raw_text, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return utterance_id\n\n    # Intent operations\n    async def create_intent(\n        self,\n        utterance_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        project_slug: str | None,\n        intent_type: str,\n        bead_ref: str | None = None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new intent and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        intent_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT INTO intents\n                   (id, utterance_id, session_id, project_slug, intent_type, bead_ref, created_at)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (intent_id, utterance_id, session_id, project_slug, intent_type, bead_ref, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return intent_id\n\n    async def update_intent_status(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        status: str,\n        resolved_at: int | None = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update intent status.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            if resolved_at is None and status == \"resolved\":\n                resolved_at = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE intents SET status = ?, resolved_at = ? WHERE id = ?\",\n                (status, resolved_at, intent_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_pending_intents(self, session_id: str) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get all pending intents for a session.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM intents\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND status IN ('pending', 'dispatched')\n                   ORDER BY created_at DESC\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    # Result operations\n    async def create_result(\n        self,\n        intent_id: str,\n        topic_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        summary: str,\n        data: dict,\n        urgency: str = \"normal\",\n        previous_result_id: str | None = None,\n        diff_summary: str | None = None,\n        diff_data: dict | None = None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new result and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        result_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT INTO results\n                   (id, intent_id, topic_id, session_id, summary, data, urgency, created_at, surfaced_at,\n                    previous_result_id, diff_summary, diff_data)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (\n                    result_id, intent_id, topic_id, session_id, summary, json.dumps(data),\n                    urgency, now, now, previous_result_id, diff_summary,\n                    json.dumps(diff_data) if diff_data else None\n                )\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return result_id\n\n    async def get_unsurfed_results(self, session_id: str) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get results that haven't been surfaced to the user yet.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM results\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND surfaced_at IS NULL\n                   ORDER BY created_at ASC\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    async def mark_results_surfed(self, session_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Mark all unsurfed results as surfaced.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE results SET surfaced_at = ? WHERE session_id = ? AND surfaced_at IS NULL\",\n                (now, session_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def mark_results_surfed_by_ids(self, session_id: str, result_ids: list[str]) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Mark specific results as surfaced.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            for result_id in result_ids:\n                await db.execute(\n                    \"UPDATE results SET surfaced_at = ? WHERE id = ? AND session_id = ?\",\n                    (now, result_id, session_id)\n                )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    # Topic operations\n    async def create_topic(\n        self,\n        label: str,\n        topic_type: str = \"adhoc\",\n        project_slugs: list[str] | None = None,\n        scope: str = \"session\",\n        session_id: str | None = None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new topic and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        topic_id = str(uuid4())\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT INTO topics\n                   (id, label, type, project_slugs, scope, session_id, created_at, last_active)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (topic_id, label, topic_type, json.dumps(project_slugs or []), scope, session_id, now, now)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return topic_id\n\n    async def find_or_create_topic(\n        self,\n        label: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        topic_type: str = \"adhoc\",\n        project_slugs: list[str] | None = None,\n    ) -> tuple[str, bool]:\n        \"\"\"Find existing topic by label and session, or create new. Returns (topic_id, created).\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            # Try to find existing topic in session scope\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT id FROM topics\n                   WHERE label = ? AND scope = 'session' AND session_id = ? AND archived_at IS NULL\n                   LIMIT 1\"\"\",\n                (label, session_id)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                if row:\n                    return row[0], False\n\n        # Create new topic\n        topic_id = await self.create_topic(label, topic_type, project_slugs, \"session\", session_id)\n        return topic_id, True\n\n    async def update_topic_activity(self, topic_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Update topic last_active timestamp.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"UPDATE topics SET last_active = ? WHERE id = ?\",\n                (now, topic_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_active_topics(self, session_id: str) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get active topics for a session, ordered by last_active.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT t.*,\n                          (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM results r WHERE r.topic_id = t.id) as result_count\n                   FROM topics t\n                   WHERE (t.session_id = ? OR t.scope IN ('cross-session', 'global'))\n                     AND t.archived_at IS NULL\n                   ORDER BY t.last_active DESC\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    async def get_latest_result_for_topic(self, topic_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get the most recent result for a topic.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT * FROM results\n                   WHERE topic_id = ?\n                   ORDER BY created_at DESC\n                   LIMIT 1\"\"\",\n                (topic_id,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                return dict(row) if row else None\n\n    async def link_intent_to_topic(self, intent_id: str, topic_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Link an intent to a topic (many-to-many).\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"INSERT OR IGNORE INTO intent_topics (intent_id, topic_id) VALUES (?, ?)\",\n                (intent_id, topic_id)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    # Workload summary for reconnect\n    async def get_workload_summary(self, session_id: str) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Get summary of what happened while surface was disconnected.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            # Get pending intents\n            pending_count = (await db.execute_fetchall(\n                \"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM intents WHERE session_id = ? AND status IN ('pending', 'dispatched')\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ))[0][0]\n\n            # Get new results (surfaced after last surface disconnect)\n            # For now, return count of results in last hour\n            one_hour_ago = int(datetime.now().timestamp()) - 3600\n            new_results = (await db.execute_fetchall(\n                \"\"\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM results\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND created_at > ?\"\"\",\n                (session_id, one_hour_ago)\n            ))[0][0]\n\n            # Get exceptions (HUMAN beads or high urgency items)\n            exceptions = (await db.execute_fetchall(\n                \"\"\"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM results\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND urgency IN ('critical', 'high') AND acked_at IS NULL\"\"\",\n                (session_id,)\n            ))[0][0]\n\n            return {\n                \"pending_intents\": pending_count,\n                \"new_results\": new_results,\n                \"unresolved_exceptions\": exceptions,\n            }\n\n    # Topic context cache operations\n    async def set_topic_context(\n        self,\n        topic_id: str,\n        context_data: dict,\n        ttl_seconds: int = 600,  # Default 10 minutes\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Cache pre-fetched context for a topic.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        expires_at = now + ttl_seconds\n\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT OR REPLACE INTO topic_context_cache\n                   (topic_id, context_data, fetched_at, expires_at)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)\"\"\",\n                (topic_id, json.dumps(context_data), now, expires_at)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_topic_context(self, topic_id: str) -> Optional[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get cached context for a topic if still valid.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT context_data, expires_at FROM topic_context_cache\n                   WHERE topic_id = ? AND expires_at > ?\"\"\",\n                (topic_id, now)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                if row:\n                    return {\n                        \"context\": json.loads(row[\"context_data\"]),\n                        \"expires_at\": row[\"expires_at\"],\n                    }\n        return None\n\n    async def invalidate_topic_context(self, topic_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Invalidate cached context for a topic.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM topic_context_cache WHERE topic_id = ?\",\n                (topic_id,)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def cleanup_expired_context(self) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Remove expired context cache entries. Returns count of deleted entries.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            cursor = await db.execute(\n                \"DELETE FROM topic_context_cache WHERE expires_at <= ?\",\n                (int(datetime.now().timestamp()),)\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n            return cursor.rowcount\n\n    async def get_active_topic_ids(self) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Get IDs of all active topics (recently active, not archived).\"\"\"\n        # Active topics are those with activity in last hour\n        one_hour_ago = int(datetime.now().timestamp()) - 3600\n\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT id FROM topics\n                   WHERE last_active > ? AND archived_at IS NULL\"\"\",\n                (one_hour_ago,)\n            ) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [row[0] for row in rows]\n\n    # Feedback signal operations\n    async def create_feedback_signal(\n        self,\n        signal_id: str,\n        signal_type: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        result_id: str | None = None,\n        topic_id: str | None = None,\n        timestamp: int | None = None,\n        data: dict | None = None,\n        surface_type: str | None = None,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Create a new feedback signal and return its ID.\"\"\"\n        import json\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            await db.execute(\n                \"\"\"INSERT INTO feedback_signals\n                   (id, signal_type, session_id, result_id, topic_id, timestamp, data, surface_type)\n                   VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, surface_type\n                )\n            )\n            await db.commit()\n        return signal_id\n\n    async def get_unprocessed_signals(\n        self,\n        signal_type: str | None = None,\n        limit: int = 100,\n    ) -> list[dict]:\n        \"\"\"Get unprocessed feedback signals for background analysis.\"\"\"\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n            query = \"\"\"SELECT * FROM feedback_signals WHERE processed = 0\"\"\"\n            params = []\n\n            if signal_type:\n                query += \" AND signal_type = ?\"\n                params.append(signal_type)\n\n            query += \" ORDER BY timestamp ASC LIMIT ?\"\n            params.append(limit)\n\n            async with db.execute(query, params) as cursor:\n                rows = await cursor.fetchall()\n                return [dict(row) for row in rows]\n\n    async def mark_signals_processed(\n        self,\n        signal_ids: list[str],\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Mark feedback signals as processed.\"\"\"\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            for signal_id in signal_ids:\n                await db.execute(\n                    \"UPDATE feedback_signals SET processed = 1, processed_at = ? WHERE id = ?\",\n                    (now, signal_id)\n                )\n            await db.commit()\n\n    async def get_session_feedback_summary(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        since: int | None = None,\n    ) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"Get a summary of feedback signals for a session.\"\"\"\n        one_hour_ago = int(datetime.now().timestamp()) - 3600\n\n        async with aiosqlite.connect(self.db_path) as db:\n            db.row_factory = aiosqlite.Row\n\n            # Get signal counts by type\n            since_clause = since or one_hour_ago\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT signal_type, COUNT(*) as count\n                   FROM feedback_signals\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND timestamp >= ?\n                   GROUP BY signal_type\"\"\",\n                (session_id, since_clause)\n            ) as cursor:\n                type_counts = {row[\"signal_type\"]: row[\"count\"] for row in await cursor.fetchall()}\n\n            # Get average ack delay\n            async with db.execute(\n                \"\"\"SELECT AVG(CAST(data->>'ack_delay_seconds' AS INTEGER)) as avg_delay\n                   FROM feedback_signals\n                   WHERE session_id = ? AND signal_type = 'ack_speed' AND timestamp >= ?\"\"\",\n                (session_id, since_clause)\n            ) as cursor:\n                row = await cursor.fetchone()\n                avg_ack_delay = row[\"avg_delay\"] if row and row[\"avg_delay\"] else 0\n\n            return {\n                \"signal_counts\": type_counts,\n                \"avg_ack_delay_seconds\": avg_ack_delay,\n            }\n\n\n# Global session store instance\n_store: SessionStore | None = None\n\n\ndef get_store(db_path: Path | None = None) -> SessionStore:\n    \"\"\"Get or create the global session store instance.\"\"\"\n    global _store\n    if _store is None:\n        if db_path is None:\n            db_path = Path(\"/home/coding/aide-de-camp/data/session.db\")\n        _store = SessionStore(db_path)\n    return _store\n","numLines":738,"startLine":1,"totalLines":738}}}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_d2778f077294432b923972d3","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t\"\"\"\n2\tSurface routing: determine which surface(s) should receive a result.\n3\t\n4\tRouting priority:\n5\t1. Origin surface (if still connected)\n6\t2. Most recently active connected surface\n7\t3. Any connected surface\n8\t4. Always-available fallback (Telegram)\n9\t\"\"\"\n10\t\n11\tfrom dataclasses import dataclass\n12\tfrom datetime import datetime, timedelta\n13\tfrom typing import Optional\n14\t\n15\tfrom ..session.store import SessionStore\n16\t\n17\t\n18\t@dataclass\n19\tclass Surface:\n20\t    \"\"\"A surface representation.\"\"\"\n21\t    id: str\n22\t    session_id: str\n23\t    type: str\n24\t    state: str\n25\t    always_available: bool\n26\t    last_seen: int\n27\t\n28\t\n29\t@dataclass\n30\tclass RouteDecision:\n31\t    \"\"\"Routing decision for a result.\"\"\"\n32\t    target_surfaces: list[Surface]\n33\t    reason: str\n34\t    fallback_used: bool = False\n35\t\n36\t\n37\tclass SurfaceRouter:\n38\t    \"\"\"Route results to appropriate surfaces based on priority rules.\"\"\"\n39\t\n40\t    # Surface idle timeout: marks surface as idle after N seconds of inactivity\n41\t    IDLE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 300  # 5 minutes\n42\t\n43\t    def __init__(self, store: SessionStore):\n44\t        self.store = store\n45\t\n46\t    async def route_result(\n47\t        self,\n48\t        session_id: str,\n49\t        origin_surface_id: str | None = None,\n50\t        urgency: str = \"normal\",\n51\t    ) -> RouteDecision:\n52\t        \"\"\"\n53\t        Determine which surface(s) should receive a result.\n54\t\n55\t        Args:\n56\t            session_id: The session the result belongs to\n57\t            origin_surface_id: The surface where the utterance originated (if known)\n58\t            urgency: Result urgency ('critical', 'high', 'normal', 'low')\n59\t\n60\t        Returns:\n61\t            RouteDecision with target surfaces and routing reason\n62\t        \"\"\"\n63\t        active_surfaces = await self.store.get_active_surfaces(session_id)\n64\t\n65\t        # Filter out idle surfaces (but keep always-available ones)\n66\t        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n67\t        recently_active = [\n68\t            s for s in active_surfaces\n69\t            if s[\"always_available\"] or (now - s[\"last_seen\"]) < self.IDLE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS\n70\t        ]\n71\t\n72\t        # Priority 1: Origin surface (if still connected)\n73\t        if origin_surface_id:\n74\t            for surface in recently_active:\n75\t                if surface[\"id\"] == origin_surface_id and surface[\"state\"] != \"disconnected\":\n76\t                    return RouteDecision(\n77\t                        target_surfaces=[Surface(**surface)],\n78\t                        reason=\"origin-surface-active\",\n79\t                    )\n80\t\n81\t        # Priority 2: Most recently active connected surface\n82\t        if recently_active:\n83\t            # Sort by last_seen descending, pick the first non-telegram unless all are telegram\n84\t            recently_active.sort(key=lambda s: s[\"last_seen\"], reverse=True)\n85\t\n86\t            # Prefer non-telegram surfaces for non-critical results\n87\t            if urgency not in (\"critical\", \"high\"):\n88\t                for surface in recently_active:\n89\t                    if surface[\"type\"] != \"telegram\":\n90\t                        return RouteDecision(\n91\t                            target_surfaces=[Surface(**surface)],\n92\t                            reason=\"most-recent-active\",\n93\t                        )\n94\t\n95\t            # For high urgency or if only telegram available\n96\t            return RouteDecision(\n97\t                target_surfaces=[Surface(**recently_active[0])],\n98\t                reason=\"most-recent-active-or-fallback\",\n99\t            )\n100\t\n101\t        # Priority 3: Any connected surface (including idle)\n102\t        if active_surfaces:\n103\t            active_surfaces.sort(key=lambda s: s[\"last_seen\"], reverse=True)\n104\t            return RouteDecision(\n105\t                target_surfaces=[Surface(**active_surfaces[0])],\n106\t                reason=\"any-connected\",\n107\t            )\n108\t\n109\t        # Origin surface (if still connected)\n2. Most recently active connected surface\n3. Any connected surface\n4. Always-available fallback (Telegram)\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom datetime import datetime, timedelta\nfrom typing import Optional\n\nfrom ..session.store import SessionStore\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass Surface:\n    \"\"\"A surface representation.\"\"\"\n    id: str\n    session_id: str\n    type: str\n    state: str\n    always_available: bool\n    last_seen: int\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass RouteDecision:\n    \"\"\"Routing decision for a result.\"\"\"\n    target_surfaces: list[Surface]\n    reason: str\n    fallback_used: bool = False\n\n\nclass SurfaceRouter:\n    \"\"\"Route results to appropriate surfaces based on priority rules.\"\"\"\n\n    # Surface idle timeout: marks surface as idle after N seconds of inactivity\n    IDLE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = 300  # 5 minutes\n\n    def __init__(self, store: SessionStore):\n        self.store = store\n\n    async def route_result(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        origin_surface_id: str | None = None,\n        urgency: str = \"normal\",\n    ) -> RouteDecision:\n        \"\"\"\n        Determine which surface(s) should receive a result.\n\n        Args:\n            session_id: The session the result belongs to\n            origin_surface_id: The surface where the utterance originated (if known)\n            urgency: Result urgency ('critical', 'high', 'normal', 'low')\n\n        Returns:\n            RouteDecision with target surfaces and routing reason\n        \"\"\"\n        active_surfaces = await self.store.get_active_surfaces(session_id)\n\n        # Filter out idle surfaces (but keep always-available ones)\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n        recently_active = [\n            s for s in active_surfaces\n            if s[\"always_available\"] or (now - s[\"last_seen\"]) < self.IDLE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS\n        ]\n\n        # Priority 1: Origin surface (if still connected)\n        if origin_surface_id:\n            for surface in recently_active:\n                if surface[\"id\"] == origin_surface_id and surface[\"state\"] != \"disconnected\":\n                    return RouteDecision(\n                        target_surfaces=[Surface(**surface)],\n                        reason=\"origin-surface-active\",\n                    )\n\n        # Priority 2: Most recently active connected surface\n        if recently_active:\n            # Sort by last_seen descending, pick the first non-telegram unless all are telegram\n            recently_active.sort(key=lambda s: s[\"last_seen\"], reverse=True)\n\n            # Prefer non-telegram surfaces for non-critical results\n            if urgency not in (\"critical\", \"high\"):\n                for surface in recently_active:\n                    if surface[\"type\"] != \"telegram\":\n                        return RouteDecision(\n                            target_surfaces=[Surface(**surface)],\n                            reason=\"most-recent-active\",\n                        )\n\n            # For high urgency or if only telegram available\n            return RouteDecision(\n                target_surfaces=[Surface(**recently_active[0])],\n                reason=\"most-recent-active-or-fallback\",\n            )\n\n        # Priority 3: Any connected surface (including idle)\n        if active_surfaces:\n            active_surfaces.sort(key=lambda s: s[\"last_seen\"], reverse=True)\n            return RouteDecision(\n                target_surfaces=[Surface(**active_surfaces[0])],\n                reason=\"any-connected\",\n            )\n\n        # Priority 4: Always-available fallback (Telegram)\n        fallback = await self.store.get_fallback_surface()\n        if fallback:\n            return RouteDecision(\n                target_surfaces=[Surface(**fallback)],\n                reason=\"always-available-fallback\",\n                fallback_used=True,\n            )\n\n        # No surface available - result will wait in queue\n        return RouteDecision(\n            target_surfaces=[],\n            reason=\"no-surface-available\",\n        )\n\n    async def route_to_all_active(\n        self,\n        session_id: str,\n        exclude_surface_id: str | None = None,\n    ) -> list[Surface]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Route to all active surfaces (for multi-surface scenarios).\n\n        Used when a user has both canvas and audio surfaces active.\n        \"\"\"\n        active_surfaces = await self.store.get_active_surfaces(session_id)\n        now = int(datetime.now().timestamp())\n\n        result = []\n        for s in active_surfaces:\n            if exclude_surface_id and s[\"id\"] == exclude_surface_id:\n                continue\n            if s[\"always_available\"] or (now - s[\"last_seen\"]) < self.IDLE_TIMEOUT_SECONDS:\n                result.append(Surface(**s))\n\n        return result\n\n    async def handle_surface_disconnect(self, surface_id: str, session_id: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"\n        Handle a surface disconnecting.\n        Marks surface as disconnected and cleans up if needed.\n        \"\"\"\n        await self.store.mark_surface_disconnected(surface_id)\n\n    async def handle_surface_reconnect(\n        self,\n        surface_id: str,\n        session_id: str,\n        surface_type: str,\n    ) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"\n        Handle a surface reconnecting after disconnection.\n\n        Returns True if this is a reconnection of an existing surface,\n        False if it's a new surface.\n        \"\"\"\n        # Check if surface exists\n        async with self.store as store:\n            surfaces = await store.get_active_surfaces(session_id)\n            for existing in surfaces:\n                if existing[\"id\"] == surface_id:\n                    # Reconnection - update heartbeat\n                    await store.update_surface_heartbeat(surface_id)\n                    return True\n\n        # New surface - register it\n        await store.register_surface(session_id, surface_type)\n        return False\n\n    async def get_workload_summary(self, session_id: str) -> dict:\n        \"\"\"\n        Get workload summary for a session (for surface reconnection).\n        \"\"\"\n        return await self.store.get_workload_summary(session_id)\n","numLines":183,"startLine":1,"totalLines":183}}}

       ]\n377\t    }\n378\t\n379\t\n380\t@app.get(\"/events\")\n381\tasync def canvas_sse(\n382\t    session_id: str,\n383\t    surface_id: str | None = None,\n384\t):\n385\t    \"\"\"\n386\t session_id: str,\n    surface_id: Optional[str] = None,\n    surface_type: str = \"canvas\"\n):\n    \"\"\"\n    Server-Sent Events endpoint for canvas connections.\n\n    Provides real-time updates for topics, results, and component updates.\n    \"\"\"\n    broadcaster = get_broadcaster()\n\n    # Get or create session\n    store = await get_store()\n    session = await    if not surface_id:\n        surface_id = await store.register_surface(session_id, surface_type)\n        logger.info(f\"Registered {surface_type} surface: {surface_id}\")\n    else:\n        await surface_type=surface_type,\n    )\n\n    # Send workload summary on connect\n    summary = await store.get_workload_summary(session_id)\n\n    async def event_stream():\n        try:\n            #           yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"workload_summary\", summary)\n\n            # Send initial topic cards\n            if _topic_manager:\n                cards = await _topic_manager.get_active_topic_cards(session_id)\n                yield broadcaster._format_sse(\"topic_cards\", {\n                    \"cards\": [card.to_dict() for card in cards]\n                })\n\n            # Stream events\n            async for event_data in broadcaster.event_generator(connection):\n                yield event_data\n\n        except asyncio.CancelledError:\n            logger.info(f\"SSE stream cancelled for surface {surface_id}\")\n            raise\n\n    return StreamingResponse(\n        event_stream(),\n        media_type=\"text/event-stream\",\n
